text.title: Rust Error Handling Decision Tree
text.summary: Routes crate type to error-library choice and variant strategy. Leaves reference rules from 01-core-rules.xml.

branch.question: Does downstream code need to `match` specific failures?

conclusion.verdict: `#[derive(thiserror::Error)]` enum with named variants + `#[from]` source chains
Single opaque variant `Other(#[from] anyhow::Error)` is acceptable, but still ship a typed enum
`anyhow::Result<T>` + `.context()` at every fallible boundary; no enum needed
`Box<dyn std::error::Error>` is fine; build.rs is single-use

text.example: crate_type=library, downstream_match=true → thiserror-for-lib conclusion. See 05-examples.xml library section. Clippy gate `unwrap_used = deny` still applies.
